---
name: 13.19-create-19-workflow-call-workflow
description: Create the 19-workflow-call GitHub Actions workshop workflow from a natural-language specification that introduces reusable workflow definitions.
---

# Create 19 Workflow Call Workflow

## Objective

Create `.github/workflows/19-workflow-call-workflow.yml` if it has been deleted or needs to be rebuilt.

This prompt is intentionally written as a natural-language workflow request rather than a literal YAML template. Use it to demonstrate how a workflow can be made reusable with `workflow_call`.

## Natural-Language Workflow Specification

Create a GitHub Actions workflow named `19-workflow-call-workflow` in `.github/workflows/19-workflow-call-workflow.yml`.

The workflow should introduce reusable workflow definitions:

- Trigger only through `workflow_call`
- Define a required string input named `report_title` with the description `Title to print from the reusable workflow`
- Configure read-only repository contents permission for `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Define one job named `reusable-report` that runs on `ubuntu-latest` with a 5-minute timeout
- Add a step named `print-reusable-report` that prints that the workflow is reusable, prints the `report_title` input, and prints the current repository from GitHub contexts

## Generation Requirements

1. Generate valid GitHub Actions YAML from the natural-language specification above.
2. Preserve the workflow name, `workflow_call` trigger, input name, input type, input requirement, input description, read-only permission, job name, timeout, runner, and step name.
3. Keep this workflow focused on defining a reusable workflow; do not add push, pull request, schedule, or manual triggers.
4. Do not add secrets or environment secrets to this reusable workflow.

## Validation

After creating the workflow, validate `.github/workflows/19-workflow-call-workflow.yml` with `actionlint` when available.

If validation cannot reach 0 errors, create `.github/workflows/logs/workflow-prompt-errors.md` and include:

- The failing workflow path
- The validation command used
- The full validation error output
- The likely cause
- Recommended fixes

Focused validation command:

```pwsh
$actionlint = Get-Command actionlint -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if ($actionlint) {
  actionlint .github/workflows/19-workflow-call-workflow.yml
}
```
